sections.heading: TNM
sections.content: {"system": "AJCC 8th/9th edition TNM staging systems (Version 9, 2023 for GI and pancreatic NETs; Version 9, 2024 for lung and thymus; 8th edition, 2017 for adrenocortical carcinoma and PCC/PGL)", "t_stages": [{"stage": "T1 (Jejunal/Ileal NET)", "description": "Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa, and ≤1 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T2 (Jejunal/Ileal NET)", "description": "Tumor invades muscularis propria or >1 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T3 (Jejunal/Ileal NET)", "description": "Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into subserosal tissue without penetration of overlying serosa"}, {"stage": "T4 (Jejunal/Ileal NET)", "description": "Tumor invades visceral peritoneum (serosal) or other organs or adjacent structures"}, {"stage": "T1 (Duodenal/Ampulla NET)", "description": "Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa only and ≤1 cm (duodenal); ≤1 cm and confined within sphincter of Oddi (ampullary)"}, {"stage": "T2 (Duodenal/Ampulla NET)", "description": "Tumor invades muscularis propria or >1 cm (duodenal); invades through sphincter into duodenal submucosa/muscularis propria or >1 cm (ampullary)"}, {"stage": "T3 (Duodenal/Ampulla NET)", "description": "Tumor invades the pancreas or peripancreatic adipose tissue"}, {"stage": "T4 (Duodenal/Ampulla NET)", "description": "Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum (serosa) or other organs"}, {"stage": "T1 (Appendiceal NET)", "description": "Tumor ≤2 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T2 (Appendiceal NET)", "description": "Tumor >2 cm but ≤4 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T3 (Appendiceal NET)", "description": "Tumor >4 cm in greatest dimension, or with subserosal invasion, or involvement of the mesoappendix"}, {"stage": "T4 (Appendiceal NET)", "description": "Tumor perforates the peritoneum, or directly invades other adjacent organs or structures (excluding direct mural extension to adjacent subserosa of adjacent bowel)"}, {"stage": "T1 (Colon/Rectum NET)", "description": "Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa and ≤2 cm; T1a ≤1 cm, T1b >1–≤2 cm"}, {"stage": "T2 (Colon/Rectum NET)", "description": "Tumor invades muscularis propria, or is >2 cm in greatest dimension with invasion of the mucosa or submucosa"}, {"stage": "T3 (Colon/Rectum NET)", "description": "Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into subserosal tissue without penetration of overlying serosa"}, {"stage": "T4 (Colon/Rectum NET)", "description": "Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum (serosa), or other organs or adjacent structures"}, {"stage": "T1 (Gastric NET)", "description": "Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa and ≤1 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T2 (Gastric NET)", "description": "Tumor invades muscularis propria or >1 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T3 (Gastric NET)", "description": "Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into subserosal tissue without penetration of overlying serosa"}, {"stage": "T4 (Gastric NET)", "description": "Tumor invades visceral peritoneum (serosa) or other organs or adjacent structures"}, {"stage": "T1 (Pancreatic NET)", "description": "Tumor limited to the pancreas, ≤2 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T2 (Pancreatic NET)", "description": "Tumor limited to the pancreas, >2 cm but ≤4 cm in greatest dimension"}, {"stage": "T3 (Pancreatic NET)", "description": "Tumor limited to the pancreas, >4 cm in greatest dimension; or tumor invading the duodenum, ampulla of Vater, or common bile duct"}, {"stage": "T4 (Pancreatic NET)", "description": "Tumor invading adjacent organs (stomach, spleen, colon, adrenal gland) or the wall of large vessels (celiac axis or the superior mesenteric artery/vein, splenic artery/vein, gastroduodenal artery/vein, portal vein)"}, {"stage": "T1 (Lung NET)", "description": "Tumor ≤3 cm; subcategories T1a (≤1 cm), T1b (>1–≤2 cm), T1c (>2–≤3 cm)"}, {"stage": "T2 (Lung NET)", "description": "Tumor >3 cm but ≤5 cm; subcategories T2a (>3–≤4 cm), T2b (>4–≤5 cm)"}, {"stage": "T3 (Lung NET)", "description": "Tumor >5 cm but ≤7 cm, or ≤7 cm with invasion of parietal pleura/chest wall, pericardium, phrenic nerve, azygos vein, thoracic nerve roots, stellate ganglion, or separate tumor nodule(s) in the same lobe"}, {"stage": "T4 (Lung NET)", "description": "Tumor >7 cm, or tumor of any size invading mediastinum, thymus, trachea, carina, recurrent laryngeal nerve, vagus nerve, esophagus, diaphragm, heart, great vessels, subclavian vessels, vertebral body, spinal canal, cervical nerve roots, brachial plexus, or separate tumor nodule(s) in a different ipsilateral lobe"}, {"stage": "T1a/T1b (Thymic NET)", "description": "T1a: ≤5 cm, limited to thymus/encapsulation/mediastinal fat/mediastinal pleura only. T1b: >5 cm, same extent."}, {"stage": "T2 (Thymic NET)", "description": "Direct invasion of pericardium (partial or full thickness), or lung, or phrenic nerve"}, {"stage": "T3 (Thymic NET)", "description": "Direct invasion into brachiocephalic vein, superior vena cava, chest wall, or extrapericardial pulmonary artery"}]}

t_stages.description: Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa, and ≤1 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor invades muscularis propria or >1 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into subserosal tissue without penetration of overlying serosa
Tumor invades visceral peritoneum (serosal) or other organs or adjacent structures
Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa only and ≤1 cm (duodenal); ≤1 cm and confined within sphincter of Oddi (ampullary)
Tumor invades muscularis propria or >1 cm (duodenal); invades through sphincter into duodenal submucosa/muscularis propria or >1 cm (ampullary)
Tumor invades the pancreas or peripancreatic adipose tissue
Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum (serosa) or other organs
Tumor ≤2 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor >2 cm but ≤4 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor >4 cm in greatest dimension, or with subserosal invasion, or involvement of the mesoappendix
Tumor perforates the peritoneum, or directly invades other adjacent organs or structures (excluding direct mural extension to adjacent subserosa of adjacent bowel)
Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa and ≤2 cm; T1a ≤1 cm, T1b >1–≤2 cm
Tumor invades muscularis propria, or is >2 cm in greatest dimension with invasion of the mucosa or submucosa
Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into subserosal tissue without penetration of overlying serosa
Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum (serosa), or other organs or adjacent structures
Tumor invades mucosa or submucosa and ≤1 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor invades muscularis propria or >1 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into subserosal tissue without penetration of overlying serosa
Tumor invades visceral peritoneum (serosa) or other organs or adjacent structures
Tumor limited to the pancreas, ≤2 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor limited to the pancreas, >2 cm but ≤4 cm in greatest dimension
Tumor limited to the pancreas, >4 cm in greatest dimension; or tumor invading the duodenum, ampulla of Vater, or common bile duct

Tumor invading adjacent organs (stomach, spleen, colon, adrenal gland) or the wall of large vessels (celiac axis or the superior mesenteric artery/vein, splenic artery/vein, gastroduodenal artery/vein, portal vein)
Tumor ≤3 cm; subcategories T1a (≤1 cm), T1b (>1–≤2 cm), T1c (>2–≤3 cm)
Tumor >3 cm but ≤5 cm; subcategories T2a (>3–≤4 cm), T2b (>4–≤5 cm)

Tumor >5 cm but ≤7 cm, or ≤7 cm with invasion of parietal pleura/chest wall, pericardium, phrenic nerve, azygos vein, thoracic nerve roots, stellate ganglion, or separate tumor nodule(s) in the same lobe

Tumor >7 cm, or tumor of any size invading mediastinum, thymus, trachea, carina, recurrent laryngeal nerve, vagus nerve, esophagus, diaphragm, heart, great vessels, subclavian vessels, vertebral body, spinal canal, cervical nerve roots, brachial plexus, or separate tumor nodule(s) in a different ipsilateral lobe
T1a: ≤5 cm, limited to thymus/encapsulation/mediastinal fat/mediastinal pleura only. T1b: >5 cm, same extent.
Direct invasion of pericardium (partial or full thickness), or lung, or phrenic nerve
Direct invasion into brachiocephalic vein, superior vena cava, chest wall, or extrapericardial pulmonary artery
